description_long: This is a large, cylindrical wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by six robust metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain patterns and some discoloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The metal bands are dark gray with patches of rust, reinforcing the structure and adding to its rustic appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top being fully closed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, indicating it is a traditional storage barrel. The overall craftsmanship and materials suggest it is designed for durability and could be used for storing liquids or dry goods in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A large, weathered wooden barrel with six dark metal hoops and a closed flat top, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, flat closed top, and all six metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the texture of the wood, with the metal bands visible in perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, again showing the bulging midsection and the weathered wood grain.
